text:
🚨 Photonics may become the most important technology shift of the next decade.

Not AI software.

Not apps.

Not even GPUs alone.

The REAL revolution may be the replacement of electricity with LIGHT inside computing infrastructure. 🧵

Silicon electronics are approaching physical limits.

Modern AI infrastructure now faces: ⚠️ bandwidth ceilings ⚠️ power-consumption explosions ⚠️ heat-density problems ⚠️ latency bottlenecks ⚠️ interconnect saturation

The current architecture is becoming unsustainable.

This is where photonics enters.

Photonics = using LIGHT (photons) instead of electricity (electrons) to: 💡 transmit data 💡 process signals 💡 move information 💡 scale compute infrastructure

The implications are enormous.

We believe photonics is no longer speculative science fiction.

It is increasingly becoming: ⚠️ necessary ⚠️ commercially viable ⚠️ strategically essential

Especially for hyperscale AI systems.

The AI boom itself is creating the problem photonics solves.

As models scale: 📈 compute demand explodes 📈 rack density rises 📈 cooling loads surge 📈 electricity consumption skyrockets

Copper interconnects increasingly become the bottleneck.

Traditional electrical systems waste massive amounts of energy as heat.

Photonics changes the equation: ⚡ lower power consumption ⚡ faster transmission ⚡ lower latency ⚡ higher bandwidth ⚡ less thermal stress

This is why hyperscalers are aggressively pursuing it.

In this case the sequence is straightforward:

Step 1: 🤖 AI models scale exponentially

Step 2: ⚠️ Electrical interconnects hit limits

Step 3: 🔥 Cooling and power costs explode

Step 4: 💡 Photonics becomes economically necessary

Step 5: 🏗 Entire data-center architecture changes

This transition is already happening quietly.

Over the next 3–5 years: ⚠️ co-packaged optics ⚠️ optical switching ⚠️ silicon photonics ⚠️ photonic networking

…will increasingly replace traditional copper infrastructure.

The implications go far beyond performance.

Photonics may fundamentally reshape: 🏭 semiconductor design ⚡ power consumption 💧 cooling infrastructure 🧠 AI deployment economics 🏢 hyperscale construction

We believe this becomes one of the clearest examples of K-shaped technological consolidation.

Why?

Because only the largest players can afford: 💰 photonic R&D 💰 custom AI infrastructure 💰 advanced packaging 💰 optical integration (Watch chip makers rotating, new partnerships)

Smaller competitors increasingly get locked out.

The photonics transition favors: ✅ hyperscalers ✅ mega-cap semiconductor firms ✅ defense-linked infrastructure players ✅ vertically integrated ecosystems

The technological moat widens.

🚨 INVESTABLE PLAYERS

Several companies already sit directly inside this transition.

⚡ Broadcom ($AVGO) ⚡ Coherent ($COHR) ⚡ Lumentum ($LITE) ⚡ Intel ($INTC) ⚡ Laser Photonics ($LASE)

Each attacks a different layer of the stack.

$AVGO is one of the strongest large-cap photonics plays.

Broadcom already dominates: 🔌 networking 🔌 optical connectivity 🔌 silicon-photonics infrastructure 🔌 AI data-center interconnects

It sits directly at the AI bandwidth bottleneck.

$COHR is another critical player.

Coherent controls: 💡 industrial lasers 💡 optical subsystems 💡 photonic components 💡 semiconductor manufacturing optics

As photonic infrastructure scales, laser demand scales with it.

$LITE remains deeply leveraged to: 📡 fiber optics 📡 telecom photonics 📡 optical sensing 📡 AI networking expansion

The company effectively monetizes bandwidth growth itself.

$INTC is quietly positioning aggressively in silicon photonics.

Intel understands a critical reality:

Traditional scaling alone is no longer enough.

Future compute increasingly depends on: 💡 optical movement of data rather than purely electrical scaling.

Smaller pure-play names like: ⚡ $LASE ⚡ Ayar Labs (private) ⚡ Lightmatter (private)

represent the more speculative frontier.

Higher risk. Potentially enormous upside.

Especially if optical interconnect adoption accelerates faster than expected.

Emphasis

In photonics the bottlenecks are: ⚠️ optical integration ⚠️ laser systems ⚠️ advanced packaging ⚠️ thermal efficiency ⚠️ networking infrastructure

The defense angle matters enormously too.

Photonics is not just about AI chatbots.

It increasingly affects: 🛰 satellite systems 🎯 targeting infrastructure 📡 radar systems ⚔ electronic warfare 🤖 autonomous drones

This is becoming strategic infrastructure.

Another major theme:

Photonics helps reduce energy intensity.

This becomes critical because hyperscale AI now collides directly with: ⚡ grid shortages 💧 water scarcity 🔥 cooling constraints 🛢 diesel stress

The physical economy increasingly constrains digital expansion.

We have repeatedly emphasized:

The future AI race may not simply be about: “Who has the best model?”

It may become: “Who can physically power and cool computation?”

Photonics helps solve that problem.

This is why the market increasingly rewards: 🏗 infrastructure ⚡ utilities 💧 cooling 🧲 critical minerals 💡 photonic efficiency

The AI story is becoming deeply industrial.

The broader implication is profound:

Photonics may ultimately do to traditional electronics… …what the internet did to analog communication.

The infrastructure layer itself changes.

But the transition also accelerates concentration.

Only a narrowing circle of: 🏢 hyperscalers 🏦 mega-cap firms 🏭 industrial suppliers ⚔ defense-linked infrastructure players

…may fully benefit.

The K-shape deepens.

The irony is striking:

The AI revolution was originally marketed as democratizing technology.

Instead it increasingly centralizes: ⚡ compute ⚡ infrastructure ⚡ capital ⚡ energy access ⚡ photonic capability

into fewer hands.

We believe the market still underestimates how quickly photonics may become unavoidable.

Because the problem is no longer theoretical.

The electrical system itself is hitting physical limits.

The final takeaway:

The next generation of AI winners may not simply be software companies.

They may be the firms controlling: 💡 light transmission ⚡ power efficiency 🔥 thermal management 🏗 optical infrastructure

The future of computing increasingly belongs to photonics.

{"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "🚨 Photonics may become the most important technology shift of the next decade. Not AI software. Not apps. Not even GPUs alone. The REAL revolution may be the replacement of electricity with LIGHT inside computing infrastructure. 🧵 Silicon electronics are approaching physical limits. Modern AI infrastructure now faces: ⚠️ bandwidth ceilings ⚠️ power-consumption explosions ⚠️ heat-density problems ⚠️ latency bottlenecks ⚠️ interconnect saturation The current architecture is becoming unsustainable. This is where photonics enters. Photonics = using LIGHT (photons) instead of electricity (electrons) to: 💡 transmit data 💡 process signals 💡 move information 💡 scale compute infrastructure The implications are enormous. We believe photonics is no longer speculative science fiction. It is increasingly becoming: ⚠️ necessary ⚠️ commercially viable ⚠️ strategically essential Especially for hyperscale AI systems. The AI boom itself is creating the problem photonics solves. As models scale: 📈 compute demand explodes 📈 rack density rises 📈 cooling loads surge 📈 electricity consumption skyrockets Copper interconnects increasingly become the bottleneck. Traditional electrical systems waste massive amounts of energy as heat. Photonics changes the equation: ⚡ lower power consumption ⚡ faster transmission ⚡ lower latency ⚡ higher bandwidth ⚡ less thermal stress This is why hyperscalers are aggressively pursuing it. In this case the sequence is straightforward: Step 1: 🤖 AI models scale exponentially Step 2: ⚠️ Electrical interconnects hit limits Step 3: 🔥 Cooling and power costs explode Step 4: 💡 Photonics becomes economically necessary Step 5: 🏗 Entire data-center architecture changes This transition is already happening quietly. Over the next 3–5 years: ⚠️ co-packaged optics ⚠️ optical switching ⚠️ silicon photonics ⚠️ photonic networking …will increasingly replace traditional copper infrastructure. The implications go far beyond performance. Photonics may fundamentally reshape: 🏭 semiconductor design ⚡ power consumption 💧 cooling infrastructure 🧠 AI deployment economics 🏢 hyperscale construction We believe this becomes one of the clearest examples of K-shaped technological consolidation. Why? Because only the largest players can afford: 💰 photonic R&D 💰 custom AI infrastructure 💰 advanced packaging 💰 optical integration (Watch chip makers rotating, new partnerships) Smaller competitors increasingly get locked out. The photonics transition favors: ✅ hyperscalers ✅ mega-cap semiconductor firms ✅ defense-linked infrastructure players ✅ vertically integrated ecosystems The technological moat widens. 🚨 INVESTABLE PLAYERS Several companies already sit directly inside this transition. ⚡ Broadcom ($AVGO) ⚡ Coherent ($COHR) ⚡ Lumentum ($LITE) ⚡ Intel ($INTC) ⚡ Laser Photonics ($LASE) Each attacks a different layer of the stack. $AVGO is one of the strongest large-cap photonics plays. Broadcom already dominates: 🔌 networking 🔌 optical connectivity 🔌 silicon-photonics infrastructure 🔌 AI data-center interconnects It sits directly at the AI bandwidth bottleneck. $COHR is another critical player. Coherent controls: 💡 industrial lasers 💡 optical subsystems 💡 photonic components 💡 semiconductor manufacturing optics As photonic infrastructure scales, laser demand scales with it. $LITE remains deeply leveraged to: 📡 fiber optics 📡 telecom photonics 📡 optical sensing 📡 AI networking expansion The company effectively monetizes bandwidth growth itself. $INTC is quietly positioning aggressively in silicon photonics. Intel understands a critical reality: Traditional scaling alone is no longer enough. Future compute increasingly depends on: 💡 optical movement of data rather than purely electrical scaling. Smaller pure-play names like: ⚡ $LASE ⚡ Ayar Labs (private) ⚡ Lightmatter (private) represent the more speculative frontier. Higher risk. Potentially enormous upside. Especially if optical interconnect adoption accelerates faster than expected. Emphasis In photonics the bottlenecks are: ⚠️ optical integration ⚠️ laser systems ⚠️ advanced packaging ⚠️ thermal efficiency ⚠️ networking infrastructure The defense angle matters enormously too. Photonics is not just about AI chatbots. It increasingly affects: 🛰 satellite systems 🎯 targeting infrastructure 📡 radar systems ⚔ electronic warfare 🤖 autonomous drones This is becoming strategic infrastructure. Another major theme: Photonics helps reduce energy intensity. This becomes critical because hyperscale AI now collides directly with: ⚡ grid shortages 💧 water scarcity 🔥 cooling constraints 🛢 diesel stress The physical economy increasingly constrains digital expansion. We have repeatedly emphasized: The future AI race may not simply be about: “Who has the best model?” It may become: “Who can physically power and cool computation?” Photonics helps solve that problem. This is why the market increasingly rewards: 🏗 infrastructure ⚡ utilities 💧 cooling 🧲 critical minerals 💡 photonic efficiency The AI story is becoming deeply industrial. The broader implication is profound: Photonics may ultimately do to traditional electronics… …what the internet did to analog communication. The infrastructure layer itself changes. But the transition also accelerates concentration. Only a narrowing circle of: 🏢 hyperscalers 🏦 mega-cap firms 🏭 industrial suppliers ⚔ defense-linked infrastructure players …may fully benefit. The K-shape deepens. The irony is striking: The AI revolution was originally marketed as democratizing technology. Instead it increasingly centralizes: ⚡ compute ⚡ infrastructure ⚡ capital ⚡ energy access ⚡ photonic capability into fewer hands. We believe the market still underestimates how quickly photonics may become unavoidable. Because the problem is no longer theoretical. The electrical system itself is hitting physical limits. The final takeaway: The next generation of AI winners may not simply be software companies. They may be the firms controlling: 💡 light transmission ⚡ power efficiency 🔥 thermal management 🏗 optical infrastructure The future of computing increasingly belongs to photonics. #Photonics #AI #Semiconductors #SiliconPhotonics #DataCenters #Broadcom #AVGO #Coherent #COHR #Lumentum #LITE #Intel #LASE #QuantumComputing #Optics #Hyperscalers #Infrastructure #ArtificialIntelligence #Computing #CriticalMinerals #EnergyCrisis #KShapedEconomy #Investing #StockMarket #TechStocks #PiggosTradingDesk @DonMiami3 @Whitelavender92 @Diablo_Blanco_3 @Marbella_Media"}}}, "rest_id": "2062948497977196803", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "158", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
============================================================
SOLIDIFICATION OF LIGHT AND PROGRAMMABLE MATTER
Connection Brief
============================================================

Solidification of Light:
- Refers to experiments making photons behave like condensed matter.
- Key breakthrough (March 2025): Italian researchers created the first
  "photonic supersolid" — light that has a rigid crystalline structure
  (solid-like) while flowing without friction (superfluid-like).
- Achieved using exciton-polaritons in semiconductor nanostructures.

Programmable Matter:
- Materials that can change physical properties (shape, density,
  optical traits, stiffness) in a controlled/programmable way.
- Includes metamaterials, shape-changing systems, and modular robotics.

Connections:
1. Light as a programmable medium: Supersolid light and polariton
   systems allow dynamic, reconfigurable quantum states controlled
   by lasers or external fields.

2. Programmable photonics overlap: Solid-light phenomena can serve
   as building blocks for adaptive optical chips, tunable lasers,
   optical neural networks, and quantum simulators.

3. Hybrid future tech: Combines exotic quantum behavior of "solid"
   light with the reconfigurability of smart materials, enabling
   potential applications in quantum computing, adaptive optics,
   and light-based programmable devices.

Current status: Mostly laboratory-scale quantum experiments, but
strong conceptual bridge toward future smart photonic materials.

References:
[1] Live Science / Nature (2025) - Photonic supersolid creation
[2] Wikipedia / ScienceAlert - Solid light and supersolids
[3] Wikipedia - Programmable matter
[4] Related works on polaritons and programmable photonics

text:
Researchers at Monash University have developed a tiny chip that could help pave the way for computers that process information using light instead of electricity. Published in the journal Nature Photonics, the study demonstrates a nanoscale device capable of generating, controlling and reading light-based signals on a single platform. The breakthrough uses an emerging technology called valleytronics, which relies on quantum properties in advanced materials to encode information. Researchers say the innovation could lead to faster computing, lower energy consumption and more efficient communication systems in the future.

Investigadores de la Universidad Monash desarrollaron un diminuto chip que podría ayudar a abrir el camino hacia computadoras capaces de procesar información utilizando luz en lugar de electricidad. Publicado en la revista Nature Photonics, el estudio demuestra un dispositivo a escala nanométrica capaz de generar, controlar y leer señales basadas en luz en una sola plataforma. El avance utiliza una tecnología emergente conocida como valleytrónica, que aprovecha propiedades cuánticas de materiales avanzados para codificar información. Los investigadores afirman que la innovación podría conducir a una computación más rápida, un menor consumo de energía y sistemas de comunicación más eficientes en el futuro.

text:
Karnataka CM @DKShivakumar on Friday met a high-level delegation from the German state of Thuringia, led by Minister-President Mario Voigt, to discuss strengthening economic ties and investment opportunities. 

Heavy Industries Minister @MBPatil said Karnataka and Thuringia can collaborate in optics, photonics, semiconductors, biotech, EV manufacturing, renewable energy and advanced manufacturing. Shivakumar highlighted opportunities in technology transfer, innovation, skill exchange and next-generation technologies. Voigt praised Karnataka’s R&D ecosystem, startup network, engineering talent, quantum technology & aerospace infrastructure.

Photonics bottlenecks go way beyond lasers. 

CPO/NPO integration, thermal mgmt, and OCS switches are key scalers. Diversify across the chain for AI cluster exposure. 

Pluggables probably start ramping in 2027, CPO follows. High TAM but watch yields, hyperscaler quals & execution risks. 

Picks & shovels win regardless.

text:
While Nasdaq is down 3%, the number one gainer on the S&P today is FedEx Freight $FDXF at +9%.

But, no one cared about this 5 days ago, because it's not related to bottlenecks in photonics.

Yet, trucking - especially LTL - is a bottleneck. Plus, the re-rating vs. $ODFL, $SAIA, and $XPO after spin.

Though, it's too boring for X, and such posts are becoming increasingly a waste of time.

This was #1 reason I've kept $FDX but sold my $UPS couple years ago. The theoretical value of the distributed stake implied an initial $FDXF price in the mid-to-high $170s range. As typical with spin-offs, they get sold and right now we're $150s. Estimated FY2026 revenue: $8.7B (largest North American LTL carrier by revenue). It carries major debt due to a $4.1B cash dividend paid to FedEx pre-spin. Old Dominion $ODFL: EV/EBITDA 25–27x, PE 43–47x. Superior operating ratio 72–75% range historically/recently and consistent execution. Saia $SAIA and $XPO: Also premium high 40+ PE, strong EV/EBITDA, with growth/margin stories. Potential $FDFX catalysts: - Immediate S&P 500 and Dow Jones Transportation Average inclusion (index buying). - Pure-play LTL focus vs. being buried inside $FDX . - Potential for margin/ROIC improvement under dedicated management and capex/IT focus. -Long-term re-rating opportunity if it narrows the efficiency gap toward $ODFL $SAIA levels. I added 50 shares simply because the 50 I got is too small. Similar to a semiconductor IPO, hard to think of a better time in recent history to do a trucking IPO/spinoff."}}}, "rest_id": "2061539530730254635", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15830", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062943437528404282", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "11206", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
PhotonVentures is a European venture capital fund dedicated to integrated photonics, investing in companies from seed to Series A and supporting their transition from early validation to scalable businesses. As a specialized deep tech investor, the fund combines capital with direct access to a global ecosystem of foundries, designers, and system integrators via PhotonDelta.

This article outlines how investors evaluate early-stage photonic chip startups, what differentiates investment-ready teams, and how companies can position themselves for scale, drawing on the perspective of PhotonVentures. It focuses on the practical signals investors look for across technology, market readiness, and ecosystem alignment.

Full interview here: https://t.co/HKQNA1CSJP

#engineering #technology #photonics #PICs

@photondelta

4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. The chain locks together: stress on any single link reprices every link above it."}}}, "rest_id": "2062502220889837836", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "267095", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062936149908213920", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "49", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$STM at a new all-time high after hours. 🥂

The craziest part is this hasn’t hit the mainstream yet. 

STM is quietly ramping high-volume production of its PIC100 silicon photonics platform (800G and 1.6T optical transceivers) for hyperscaler AI clusters, with plans to quadruple capacity by 2027￼ backed by long-term customer reservations. 

The anchor underneath it all is a multi-year, multi-billion dollar AWS engagement ￼spanning connectivity, microcontrollers, and power ICs, which even came with warrants to AWS for up to 24.8 million ST shares ￼ that vest as AWS buys product.

$VRAX (Virax Biolabs) — +20.20%  
$MI (NFT Limited) — +18.00%  
$LASE (Laser Photonics) — +17.77%

 Top After-Hours Losers:  $JZ (Jianzhi Education) — -22.86%  
$NRXP (NRx Pharmaceuticals) — -22.08%  
$MYND (https://t.co/azHZg4Qaxk) — -17.17%  
$ELOG — -16.31%  
$YEXT — -15.68%

Key Takeaway:
Today’s after-hours action was dominated by micro-cap and small-cap names with very low floats, leading to exaggerated percentage moves. $XOS stands out with an incredible 132% surge — likely driven by major news or contract announcement.  These kinds of moves are high-risk, high-reward and often reverse quickly at the open. Always check volume, catalysts, and news before jumping in.  Trading Advice:  Use limit orders and tight risk management  
Many of these names have extremely low liquidity  
Watch for continuation or reversal in pre-market tomorrow

text: @ByrneHobart Photonics have already been discovered

حفلة اليوم برعاية سهم $LASE 🔥🔥

Laser Photonics Corporation

▪️ارتفاع ( 160% في يوم واحد ) 🚀

▪️الأسباب الرئيسية لانفجار السهم:

✅ تم اختيار نظام مكافحة الدرونز ضمن برنامج حكومي دفاعي ( أهم خبر ) 🔥
✅ شراء عضو مجلس إدارة من السوق المفتوح قبل شهر 32,000 سهم
✅ طلبية من Johnson & Johnson 
✅ قيمة سوقية صغيرة جداً (~80 مليون دولار)

❌ لا يوجد حتى الآن عقد رسمي من وزارة الدفاع
❌ طلبية J&J صغيرة نسبياً (250 ألف دولار فقط)

▪️نشاطها : 
- أنظمة الليزر الصناعية
- إزالة الصدأ والتنظيف بالليزر
- أنظمة دفاعية مضادة للدرونز (LSAD)
- تطبيقات طبية عبر شركة CMS Laser التابعة لها

▪️الخلاصة : 

السوق لا يسعر ما حدث اليوم فقط…
بل احتمال أن تتحول شركة صغيرة قيمتها 80 مليون دولار إلى شركة دفاعية تمتلك عقوداً حكومية مستقبلاً

ملاحظة : السهم غير شرعي ، فقط مشاركة معلومات للسهم

text:
水曜朝ニュース（6/3 JST）

AIデータセンター、GPUだけ見てると次を取り逃がしそう。

$NVDA はVera Rubinで tokens per MW を前面に。$MRVL は「次のボトルネックはconnectivity」と整理。NVIDIAもSpectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsで電力効率5倍を打ち出した。

TrendForceは2027年HBM4契約価格の大幅上昇観測。$MU には追い風。

text:
마벨(Marvell) CEO, "구리의 벽(Copper Wall)이 랙 내부로 이동하고 있으며, 이를 해결할 유일한 방법은 공동 패키징 광학(CPO)뿐"이라고 밝혀

*마벨 테크놀로지(Marvell Technology)의 CEO 맷 머피(Matt Murphy)는 "AI 인프라의 다음 주요 병목 현상은 연산(Compute)이나 메모리가 아니라, 바로 연결성(Connectivity)

*머피 CEO는 구리(Copper)에서 광학(Optical) 인터커넥트로의 전환이 먼 미래가 아닌 '지금 당장' 일어나고 있으며, 이는 반도체 산업 역사상 전례가 없을 정도로 거대한 수요의 물결을 일으킬 것이라고 말했음.

*구리의 벽(Copper Wall)의 물리학

- 신호가 구리 케이블을 통해 이동할 수 있는 거리는 대역폭에 반비례

- 레인당 100Gbps 수준: 구리 케이블은 약 5미터까지 도달할 수 있어 하나의 랙(Rack) 내부에서 서버들을 연결하기에 충분.

- ​레인당 200Gbps 수준: 그 거리는 약 2.5미터로 줄어들며, 이는 랙의 높이와 내부 라우팅 요구 사항을 감안할 때 딱 한계치에 해당.

- ​레인당 400Gbps 수준: 현재 업계가 나아가고 있는 이 단계에서는 구리선으로 랙 내부를 완전히 연결하는 것이 더 이상 불가능.

*구리의 벽이 한 단계 이동할 때마다, 광학으로 전환해야 하는 연결의 수는 최소 10배 이상 증가. 머피 CEO는 이를 광학 산업의 '수요 폭발'로 묘사.

*​공동 패키징 광학(CPO): 왜 필요한가?

- 랙 내부에서의 전환은 랙과 랙 사이(Inter-rack)에 사용되는 기존의 광학 기술로는 해결할 수 없다. 랙 내부의 연결 수는 랙 간 연결 수보다 약 10배 더 많음. 이 정도의 밀도에서 표준 광학 모듈을 장착하는 것은 전력과 공간 부족으로 인해 물리적으로 불가능.

- ​CPO는 광 엔진(Optical Engines)을 스위치나 연산 패키지에 직접 통합함으로써 이 문제를 해결. 광섬유를 전면 패널이 아닌 칩(Chip)으로 직접 가져오는 방식. 이 기술은 최첨단 CMOS 공정, 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh), 첨단 패키징, 그리고 광 인터커넥트를 하나의 긴밀하게 통합된 시스템으로 결합.

- 엔비디아(Nvidia)의 베라 루빈(Vera Rubin) 플랫폼이 이미 생산 단계의 첫 CPO 기반 스위치인 '스펙트럼-X 이더넷 포토닉스(Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics)'를 도입했다는 점을 언급하며, 이러한 전환이 더 이상 이론에 머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시.

*오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨.

*​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨.

*머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조.

벽(Copper Wall)이 랙 내부로 이동하고 있으며, 이를 해결할 유일한 방법은 공동 패키징 광학(CPO)뿐"이라고 밝혀 *마벨 테크놀로지(Marvell Technology)의 CEO 맷 머피(Matt Murphy)는 "AI 인프라의 다음 주요 병목 현상은 연산(Compute)이나 메모리가 아니라, 바로 연결성(Connectivity) *머피 CEO는 구리(Copper)에서 광학(Optical) 인터커넥트로의 전환이 먼 미래가 아닌 '지금 당장' 일어나고 있으며, 이는 반도체 산업 역사상 전례가 없을 정도로 거대한 수요의 물결을 일으킬 것이라고 말했음. *구리의 벽(Copper Wall)의 물리학 - 신호가 구리 케이블을 통해 이동할 수 있는 거리는 대역폭에 반비례 - 레인당 100Gbps 수준: 구리 케이블은 약 5미터까지 도달할 수 있어 하나의 랙(Rack) 내부에서 서버들을 연결하기에 충분. - ​레인당 200Gbps 수준: 그 거리는 약 2.5미터로 줄어들며, 이는 랙의 높이와 내부 라우팅 요구 사항을 감안할 때 딱 한계치에 해당. - ​레인당 400Gbps 수준: 현재 업계가 나아가고 있는 이 단계에서는 구리선으로 랙 내부를 완전히 연결하는 것이 더 이상 불가능. *구리의 벽이 한 단계 이동할 때마다, 광학으로 전환해야 하는 연결의 수는 최소 10배 이상 증가. 머피 CEO는 이를 광학 산업의 '수요 폭발'로 묘사. *​공동 패키징 광학(CPO): 왜 필요한가? - 랙 내부에서의 전환은 랙과 랙 사이(Inter-rack)에 사용되는 기존의 광학 기술로는 해결할 수 없다. 랙 내부의 연결 수는 랙 간 연결 수보다 약 10배 더 많음. 이 정도의 밀도에서 표준 광학 모듈을 장착하는 것은 전력과 공간 부족으로 인해 물리적으로 불가능. - ​CPO는 광 엔진(Optical Engines)을 스위치나 연산 패키지에 직접 통합함으로써 이 문제를 해결. 광섬유를 전면 패널이 아닌 칩(Chip)으로 직접 가져오는 방식. 이 기술은 최첨단 CMOS 공정, 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh), 첨단 패키징, 그리고 광 인터커넥트를 하나의 긴밀하게 통합된 시스템으로 결합. - 엔비디아(Nvidia)의 베라 루빈(Vera Rubin) 플랫폼이 이미 생산 단계의 첫 CPO 기반 스위치인 '스펙트럼-X 이더넷 포토닉스(Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics)'를 도입했다는 점을 언급하며, 이러한 전환이 더 이상 이론에 머물지 않는다는 증거로 제시. *오늘날의 AI 서버는 구리를 통한 고대역폭 통신을 위해 모든 부품이 서로 가까이 위치해야 하므로 CPU, GPU, 맞춤형 가속기, 메모리, 네트워크 인터페이스가 고정된 구성으로 묶여 있음. 이로 인해 연산 장치 대 메모리의 비율이 배포 시점에 고정되어, 어떤 작업(Workload)이 주어지든 시스템의 일부 자원은 항상 유휴 상태(Underutilized)로 남게 됨. *​그러나 서버 내부의 연결이 광학으로 바뀌면 거리가 더 이상 아키처를 제약하지 않음. 연산, 메모리, 네트워킹을 각각 독립된 풀(Pool)로 분리(Disaggregated)한 뒤, 각 작업이 실제로 필요로 하는 바에 따라 동적으로 구성(Composed)할 수 있게 됨. *머피 CEO는 "사상 처음으로 설계자들은 인터커넥트(연결)의 한계가 아니라, 오직 모델의 필요에 맞춰 AI 시스템을 설계할 수 있게 될 것입니다"라고 강조. https://t.co/VkQZnIMKUB"}}}, "rest_id": "2061919430054117501", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": text:
AI depends on physical infrastructure: chips, photonics, optical interconnects and data centers. Our new article explores where amorphous fluoropolymers may fit in the AI value chain.

https://t.co/qMKbgEzsSR

#AI #Photonics #Materials

Vad jag vet har Sivers aldrig haft något PM med GF inom Photonics. Så egentligen är det första gången det blir officiellt. Men så klar,  kunde man haft med en notis och att man varit ecosystem partners på olika sätt. Dock är det nog nedan OCI MSA partners (som inte heller nämn), som egentligen gör nyheten extra intressant.

text:
Portfolio changed since the last public list.

Main changes: added TEAM/NFLX starters, trimmed IREN, removed NOWL/VIAV/SOXS, and put a tiny SOXL sleeve back on. Also added some NBIS/NVDA/META/HLIT/CEG/ETHA exposure. Translation: still risk-on research, but less "spray and pray semis" than it looks.

My core holdings:

Fintech / software / AI apps — $HOOD, NOW, CRWD, RDDT, PLTR, LMND, TEAM, CRM, NFLX, SG

AI datacenter / compute — NBIS, IREN, NVDA, AMZN, META, HIVE, SANM, OSS

Memory / semis — MU, DRAM, MRAM, TSM, PDFS, CAMT, ONTO, AOSL

Photonics / networking — COHR, LITE, AAOI, GLW, HLIT

AI power — FLNC, CEG, VST, NVTS, ENPH

Crypto / beta sleeves — IBIT, ETHA, XRP, SOXL

Most of my research here is around photonics, memory, AI infra bottlenecks, and power. But the actual portfolio is broader: fintech, software, cyber, consumer internet, and a few tactical sleeves.

The house rule right now is boring on purpose: hold the real proof winners, don't add broad beta just because something got nuked, and only press when the proof or price actually improves.

We'll see which ones earn their way in

Think of it like adding more appliances to your house without rewiring anything. You can do it up to a point. Rubin Ultra in 2027 hits that point. 576 GPU dies per rack pulling 600 kilowatts is physically impossible to run on the old wiring. So NVIDIA splits the rack into two separate units. One rack holds all the GPUs. A dedicated sidecar rack next to it handles all the power conversion at 800 volts DC. In the old system a 5kW power supply unit cost around $95 of semiconductor content. In the new 800V sidecar each unit is rated at 30kW and costs around $880 of semiconductor content. The per-unit cost goes up nearly 10x because the chips required to handle 800V cannot be made from regular silicon. You need silicon carbide and gallium nitride, which are more advanced and more expensive materials. Multiply that across an entire rack and you go from $20,186 of power semiconductors per rack at Rubin to $123,092 at Rubin Ultra. That gap does not go to $NVDA. It does not go to the companies building the racks. It goes entirely to the chip companies supplying the power semiconductors. text:
TSMC fumbles Copackaged optics for the Nth time like some fucking donkeys and now the whole industry is limping towards NPO, and the pod bros who price the entire AI TAM off Nvidia’s BOM line items still can’t actually explain what the problem is. So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will.

The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing.

TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. 

There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL.

Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics.

People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production.

My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE.

text:
구리에서 빛으로, 반도체의 한계를 넘는 여정
오늘 대화에서는 고성능 반도체 패키징 기술, 특히 HBM(High Bandwidth Memory)의 현재와 미래에 대해 깊이 있는 이야기를 나누었습니다.
현재 고속 연산을 담당하는 HBM은 D램을 여러 층 쌓아 올리고, 'TSV(Through-Silicon Via)'라는 미세한 구리 통로를 통해 데이터를 수직으로 전송합니다. 이것은 나노미터 단위의 초정밀 공정이 만들어낸 기적 같은 기술이지만, 데이터 전송량이 늘어날수록 전력 소모와 발열이라는 물리적 한계에 부딪히고 있습니다.
과거 우리가 지구촌 인터넷망을 연결할 때 전송 효율을 위해 구리선에서 광섬유로 대전환했던 것처럼, 반도체 패키징의 세계도 결국 '전기'가 아닌 '빛'으로 데이터를 전송하는 광학 기술(CPO, Silicon Photonics)의 시대로 향할 것입니다.
하지만 현재 양산 단계에서는 '구리'를 이용한 전기적 연결이 여전히 가장 현실적이고 신뢰성 높은 최선의 방식입니다. 우리는 지금 구리의 시대가 저물고, 빛의 시대가 시작되는 그 기술적 전환점의 한복판에 서 있습니다. 전력과 열이라는 인류의 과제를 해결하기 위한 새로운 소재와 구조 혁신이 계속되고 있는 것입니다.

'TSV(Through-Silicon Via)'라는 미세한 구리 통로를 통해 데이터를 수직으로 전송합니다. 이것은 나노미터 단위의 초정밀 공정이 만들어낸 기적 같은 기술이지만, 데이터 전송량이 늘어날수록 전력 소모와 발열이라는 물리적 한계에 부딪히고 있습니다. 과거 우리가 지구촌 인터넷망을 연결할 때 전송 효율을 위해 구리선에서 광섬유로 대전환했던 것처럼, text:
$SIVE delivers another very interesting move today, +13.45% (close 89.40 SEK), approaching the upper resistance zone marked at 85–90 SEK — after an aggressive rejection off the support zone at 74.50–78.50 SEK. 

The name continues to trade very extended, with a YTD return exceeding +2,000%, but that didn't stop me from taking exposure sub-40 SEK and DCA-ing at 60–65 SEK. 

The company has serious potential — recently discovered and now being accumulated by institutionals: JPMorgan disclosed a 5.25% stake in the company, its first-ever position in the stock. 

Having institutionals on our side confirms a lot. Imagine this name eventually listing on Nasdaq — access opens dramatically wider for investment funds and everyone who has missed the move so far. 

There are plenty of bearish theories that say it'll round-trip back to where it came from, but I'm very satisfied with the decision so far and will hold it for a longer period until I'm satisfied with the gain — currently +160%+, with higher targets in mind. 

The photonics names have momentum again — they keep getting bought after every dip, which suggests the move can continue.

text:
"North America sales halved purely from US defense budget approvals slipping into H2"

--> Sivers' photonics division doesn't have material U.S. defense photonics revenue to lose. Their photonics business is a LiDAR/CPO play - automotive and datacom, not defense radar or military satcom. The CHIPS Act award applied to their wireless/RF division, not photonics. Photonics revenue was DOWN 32% from last year. 

--> The opportunity pipeline is meaningless word salad. 

--> Speaking of dilution, we happen to know exactly when and where the next round is! On June 15, at their Annual General Meeting, the board will pass a 15% dilution round. 

If you absolutely must buy into this amazing "growth" story, probably wait until that passes. 

Good luck!

Opened a small position in $META. Looking into OTM leaps. 

$AMZN Bought OTM leap. Hoping for some consolidation around $240 / 245'ish. 

$CRDO decent price action on this name thus far. 
$AAOI so far I've only traded it ST twice 170+ to $200+ rinse & repeat. Options look bullish, 

 I like the sector going into end of year so any name in CPO's photonics would be a good choice. I'll personally be a bit more patient while building out positions as the MAG7 have shown some weakness lately and retesting some levels that would give us some indication about the next few months.

text:
$ASYS vs $VECO 

Which one's better?

Here's a review:

$ASYS just printed 31% y/y revenue growth in its latest quarter, expanded gross margins to 47.7% on the shift to higher-ASP AI packaging products, and moved solidly back into profitability. It trades at roughly 4x sales.

$VECO trades at a similar 3.7-4x sales. It is larger ($2.4B market cap vs $320M), more established, and carries exposure to the hot silicon photonics wave for AI optical interconnects. Both operate in the advanced packaging and process equipment layer that enables AI GPUs, chiplets, and photonics.

So why nearly identical multiples when one is smaller, growing faster in the core AI segment right now, and printing much higher gross margins?

Let's walk through it.

$ASYS specializes in thermal processing solutions: precision reflow ovens, belt furnaces, and related tools that handle the critical solder reflow and thermal steps in advanced semiconductor packaging for AI GPUs, chiplets, and high-power devices. This is the specialized “assembly oven” layer that enables high-yield, high-throughput packaging of complex multi-die architectures.

$VECO offers a broader set of process tools: wet processing/clean/etch platforms (recently qualified for new advanced packaging applications), laser spike annealing, ion beam deposition, plus MOCVD and tools supporting silicon photonics fabrication. Complementary steps in the packaging and photonics manufacturing flow.

They are not direct one-for-one competitors on every product, but they sit adjacent in the advanced packaging equipment supply chain for AI. Leading OSATs and IDMs building next-gen AI infrastructure often use tools from both (plus others) to complete the packaging and optical engine builds. You need robust thermal/reflow capability as part of the flows that VECO’s wet/anneal tools support in other steps.

In fact, strong AI packaging demand is pulling on the entire equipment ecosystem.

Here is why the market prices them so closely:

$VECO is the bigger, more diversified platform with scale across semiconductor, silicon photonics (AI optical I/O), data storage recovery, and scientific markets. More institutional coverage, better liquidity, and a “portfolio” story that captures multiple AI infrastructure vectors.

$ASYS is the smaller, more concentrated pure-play on the thermal/reflow step specifically for AI advanced packaging. Cleaner narrative, less exposed to cyclical data storage, but it carries the natural microcap liquidity and coverage discount.

$ASYS has already executed the mix shift. AI-related revenue is now >30% of its Thermal Processing segment and expected to exceed 40% soon, driving gross margins from deeply negative territory last year to 47%+ today with high incremental margins as volume scales.

$ASYS runs an asset-light model with focused subsidiaries and recently raised capital to accelerate growth and M&A. Order momentum and backlog are building on AI packaging demand.

That explains a lot of the similarity in multiples. But here is what $ASYS brings that creates the opportunity:

- Superior margin profile and operating leverage right now. The AI packaging reflow products carry high ASPs and high gross margins. As that mix climbs further, margins expand with limited opex growth.

- More direct, leveraged exposure to the AI GPU/chiplet packaging capex wave. This is one of the most capacity-constrained and critical parts of the AI buildout. Advanced thermal processes are essential for yield and reliability in high-density, high-power packages.

- Smaller base + re-rating torque. Proving sustained growth and 45%+ gross margins in a high-demand niche can support a move to 6-8x sales (still reasonable for this profile). That would be substantial upside from current levels. VECO already prices in more of its scale and photonics breadth.

- Clean U.S.-based positioning serving leading global packaging houses ramping AI products, with no China manufacturing overhang that affects some other names in the broader chain.

The takeaway and my thoughts:

I am not saying $ASYS deserves a massive premium to $VECO (but I do think this is the case lol). Scale, diversification (including photonics), and platform breadth at VECO justify a similar multiple.

But two names in the AI advanced packaging/photonics equipment ecosystem, both around 4x sales, where the smaller one is growing faster in its core AI segment, has dramatically higher and still-expanding gross margins, a cleaner focused story, and more asymmetric upside from here… that is worth sitting with.

$ASYS also has a more concentrated bet on the exact packaging equipment step seeing intense pull from every major AI accelerator designer moving to chiplet architectures.

What do you think?

Do you think $ASYS offers better risk/reward than $VECO (the larger peer in the same niche)?

I certainly do.

$ASYS is also breaking out of a 30 year support/resistance channel looking to breakout and use this as support.

$VECO already had its breakout (still seems like it wants to go up, just not as much as $ASYS here).

That's why I am long $ASYS.

If you guys got any other tickers that you want me to compare to each other, send them down below.

text:
$AAOI - Zoom out to the prize

How fast is the CPO market $AAOI feeds actually growing?

Per Yole Group, the most respected analyst in photonics, pure co-packaged optics scales from ~$46M (2024) to ~$8.1B (2030). 

That’s a 137% CAGR - one of the steepest curves anywhere in AI infrastructure (~176× in six years).

A vertically integrated laser/light-source supplier sitting dead center of that ramp is exactly the kind of name you want to own early. 

(Source: Yole Group — “Co-Packaged Optics for Data Centers 2025.”)

text:
MY PORTFOLIO EXPLAINED
I have been meticulously building a portfolio based on the thesis that the Physical AI revolution is upon us.
I have chosen each of these companies, both for what they do and for providing asymmetrical return opportunity to investors. 

$SIVE  Sivers Semiconductors 
Sivers makes high-frequency radio frequency (RF) millimeter-wave (mmWave) transceivers and advanced optical semiconductors through its Wireless and Photonics divisions. In the AI ecosystem, the company is a critical enabler of next-generation data centers, collaborating with foundry giants like GlobalFoundries to integrate its laser arrays into silicon photonics and co-packaged optics (CPO) platforms to eliminate interconnect bandwidth bottlenecks for massive workloads. Concurrently, its mmWave technologies supply high-speed wireless connectivity for satellite communications, 5G telecom, and edge infrastructure, providing the high-throughput network backbone necessary for real-time edge AI coordination and smart cities.  

$LPK LPKF Laser & Electronics 
LPKF makes high-precision, laser-based micro-material manufacturing systems, headlined by its proprietary Laser Induced Deep Etching (LIDE) technology. As conventional organic substrates struggle with heavy thermal loads and warpage from massive chiplet integration, LPKF provides the precise tools needed to drill defect-free through-glass vias (TGVs) at aspect ratios up to 1:50 with zero micro-cracks. This specialized capability directly underpins advanced hardware and high-performance computing, enabling leading chipmakers to scale up substrate sizes, integrate high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and implement in-package microfluidic cooling for the next wave of physical AI chips.  

$ACCON Acconeer
Acconeer makes ultra-low-power, miniature 60 GHz Pulsed Coherent Radar (PCR) sensors that fuse millimeter-level depth precision with an exceptionally small spatial footprint. Within Edge AI and robotics, Acconeer’s chips allow tiny, battery-constrained hardware to execute real-time motion tracking, presence detection, and material or surface classification without relying on heavy cloud computation or invasive optical cameras. By deploying deep learning interfaces that compile models directly onto its integrated microcontrollers, the company provides robots, drones, and consumer devices with the localized spatial awareness required for autonomous navigation, gesture control, and power-efficient smart presence sensing.  

$SILC  Silicom 
Silicom designs and manufactures high-performance networking and data infrastructure hardware, including server network interface cards (NICs), programmable FPGA-based acceleration cards, SmartNICs, and edge switches. The company anchors Edge AI and smart systems by offloading heavy encryption, data compression, and packet-routing tasks from host CPUs, allowing decentralized AI infrastructure to maintain ultra-low latency and maximum data throughput. Operating primarily via design wins with cybersecurity firms, hyperscalers, and telcos, Silicom enables the secure, low-latency processing fabrics required for distributed AI inference networks and 5G/6G open radio access network (O-RAN) rollouts.  

$GAPW-B Gapwaves
Gapwaves develops and fabricates specialized, millimeter-wave antennas utilizing its patented gap-waveguide technology. This low-loss, cost-efficient hardware is engineered to maximize the resolution, beam direction, and overall range of high-frequency radar systems without increasing power consumption. Gapwaves fits squarely into the autonomous systems and smart city landscapes by supplying Tier-1 automotive partners with the structural building blocks for next-generation ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems), enabling autonomous vehicles, industrial transport robots, and urban traffic monitors to build reliable, high-definition models of their surroundings under any weather condition.

$SEYE Smart Eye
Smart Eye makes computer-vision hardware and software platforms dedicated to Human Insight AI, tracking eye gaze, facial expressions, and head movements in real time. The company’s primary footprint is in the automotive sector, where its edge-based Driver Monitoring Systems (DMS) and interior cabin sensing modules identify operator distraction, drowsiness, and cognitive load to comply with global safety mandates. This technology bridges the human-machine interface across smart transportation and autonomous vehicles, while its subsidiaries (like Affectiva) extend this technology into emotion AI, which is increasingly utilized in behavioral research, aerospace simulators, and interactive robotics.

$AMBA Ambarella
Ambarella builds ultra-low-power, high-efficiency system-on-chip (SoC) vision and radar processors built on its algorithm-first CVflow artificial intelligence architecture. Its chips, like the 4nm CV7 and the high-compute N1 series, serve as the localized brain for Edge AI, industrial robotics, and smart city infrastructure by running complex convolutional and transformer neural networks simultaneously. By integrating advanced image signal processing, hardware-accelerated video encoding, and native neural network computation on a single die, Ambarella allows security cameras, aerial drones, and autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) to execute edge-based multi-sensor perception, visual odometry, and real-time SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) entirely disconnected from the cloud.

$OUST Ouster
 Ouster designs and manufactures high-resolution 3D digital LiDAR sensors alongside integrated perception suites, following its strategic combination with Stereolabs' ZED camera technology. By embedding native color and precise spatial mapping directly onto its proprietary silicon architecture, Ouster creates an end-to-end sensing platform for Physical AI. This hardware acts as the primary sensory organ for humanoid robotics, warehouse automation, autonomous mining fleets, and smart city infrastructure (via its BlueCity traffic management software), delivering low-latency, resilient environmental data that allows machines to map, track, and safely navigate complex physical spaces.

$AMZN Amazon
Within the AI and robotics verticals, Amazon develops custom infrastructure silicon—such as its proprietary Trainium and Inferentia acceleration chips—while engineering vast, automated logistics systems for its global fulfillment network. The company is a pioneer in warehouse robotics, deploying fleets of fully autonomous mobile robots like Proteus and intelligent manipulation systems like Sparrow to streamline its supply chain via machine learning and reinforcement learning. On the edge, Amazon implements localized AI and natural language processing through consumer ecosystems like Alexa, Ring, and the Astro home robot, establishing a ubiquitous presence across cloud AI architecture, industrial automation, and smart home systems.

$ELMT Elmet Group
Elmet Group fabricates precision-engineered components and high-power radio frequency (RF) or microwave equipment, operating through its Critical Materials Components and Engineered Microwave Products divisions. Specializing in the custom manufacturing of refractory metals like pure tungsten and molybdenum, Elmet supplies the foundational, high-durability hardware components required for advanced semiconductor fabrication, aerospace systems, and defense electronics. It fits into the advanced hardware and AI supply chain by delivering the critical thermal management substrates, ion implantation shielding, and robust RF distribution modules necessary to manufacture and run high-energy compute chips.

$TRT Trio-Tech International 
Trio-Tech provides semiconductor back-end testing services and manufactures heavy-duty reliability equipment, including customized burn-in boards, wet process stations, and environmental testing chambers. The company serves as a vital gatekeeper for advanced hardware reliability, validating that complex chips can withstand severe thermal and electrical stress without failing prior to commercial rollout. Driven by the massive ramp-up in next-generation computing architectures, Trio-Tech directly supports AI and smart automotive expansion by stress-testing high-performance GPUs, CPUs, and electric vehicle (EV) electronics across its key facilities in Southeast Asia.

$HLIT Harmonic
Harmonic produces virtualized broadband access networks and software-based cloud video delivery infrastructure, known for solutions like its VOS360 cloud platform and cOS applications. The company embeds AI into smart systems by leveraging Content-Aware Encoding (CAE) algorithms and dynamic ad-insertion technologies to process mass media streams at the network edge, squeezing bandwidth requirements by up to 50% without compromising visual quality. This positions Harmonic as a fundamental utility provider for edge systems and smart cities, optimizing the heavy data pipelines that distribute live television, ultra-high-definition streaming, and localized broadband connectivity at scale.

text:
For those just starting to follow $SIVE, here's a breakdown of their previous earnings report 

On the surface it looked rough.

But underneath it was a 2027 story. Quick breakdown

The "ugly":

Revenue 61.9m SEK, down 22% YoY
Gross margin caved to 6.8% (was 11.7%)
Adj EBITDA -13.8m, worse than last year
Cash down to 26.6m from 73.5m a year ago

They burned ~49m in operating cash in a single quarter. That’s the real headline.

It gets spicier. To stay funded they:

Raised ~125m SEK in April (more dilution, share count 269m to 311m YoY)
Took a $17m loan facility at 12% / 10.85%

That is expensive money. The balance sheet quality got worse this quarter.

But here’s why I’m still holding:

The revenue miss is timing, not lost business. North America sales halved purely from US defense budget approvals slipping into H2. Europe and Asia actually grew. Demand isn’t the problem.

The bull case is the pipeline:

Opportunity pipeline up 77% YTD to $799M

Jabil collab on a 1.6T pluggable transceiver (AI datacenter validation)
LIDAR auto ramp on track for Q4 2026
Multiple 2027 ramps stacking: FWA, defense, SATCOM

So the setup is simple. This quarter they got paid in pipeline and diluted in equity at the same time.

The whole thesis rides on the 2027 ramps actually converting to orders. If they slip the way Q1 revenue did, the cash position forces more dilution at worse terms

Now post earnings, we've gotten more validation. Still speculative but derisked. 

GFS deal: SIVE’s lasers are now the reference design inside GFS’s silicon photonics platform. So when GFS sells photonics chips to data center customers, SIVE rides along as the light source. Big validation, but no confirmed revenue yet.

Ayar x NVIDIA: Ayar’s CPO just got plugged into NVIDIA’s NVLink Fusion ecosystem. SIVE makes the lasers inside Ayar’s SuperNova product. So NVIDIA scales AI factories → Ayar ships more CPO modules → SIVE ships more lasers. Classic upstream pull-through.

But underneath it was a 2027 story. Quick breakdown The "ugly": Revenue 61.9m SEK, down 22% YoY Gross margin caved to 6.8% (was 11.7%) Adj EBITDA -13.8m, worse than last year Cash down to 26.6m from 73.5m a year ago They burned ~49m in operating cash in a single quarter. That’s the real headline. It gets spicier. To stay funded they: Raised ~125m SEK in April (more dilution, share count 269m to 311m YoY) Took a $17m loan facility at 12% / 10.85% That is expensive money. The balance sheet quality got worse this quarter. But here’s why I’m still holding: The revenue miss is timing, not lost business. North America sales halved purely from US defense budget approvals slipping into H2. Europe and Asia actually grew. Demand isn’t the problem. The bull case is the pipeline: Opportunity pipeline up 77% YTD to $799M Jabil collab on a 1.6T pluggable transceiver (AI datacenter validation) LIDAR auto ramp on track for Q4 2026 Multiple 2027 ramps stacking: FWA, defense, SATCOM So the setup is simple. This quarter they got paid in pipeline and diluted in equity at the same time. The whole thesis rides on the 2027 ramps actually converting to orders. If they slip the way Q1 revenue did, the cash position forces more dilution at worse terms Now post earnings, we've gotten more validation. Still speculative but derisked. GFS deal: SIVE’s lasers are now the reference design inside GFS’s silicon photonics platform. So when GFS sells photonics chips to data center customers, SIVE rides along as the light source. Big validation, but no confirmed revenue yet. Ayar x NVIDIA: Ayar’s CPO just got plugged into NVIDIA’s NVLink Fusion ecosystem. SIVE makes the lasers inside Ayar’s SuperNova product. So NVIDIA scales AI factories → Ayar ships more CPO modules → SIVE ships more lasers. Classic upstream pull-through."}}}, "rest_id": "2064061784655626519", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "5565", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Yeah… I think all your upstream semi supply chain companies are going much higher.

Goldman now expects a combined $5.3 trillion of capex spending for the four largest hyperscalers $GOOGL / $META / MSFT / $AMZN from 2025 to 2030.

Revised up from $4.5T from Q1 earnings.

“Aggregate capex est. $7.6 trillion between 2026 and 2031.”

And it flows upward to these tiny chokepoints like $SIVE for CPO lasers/ $SOI for Silicon Photonics substrates. Leaderdrive/Harmonic for Humanoids components.

And so on…

Ai names don’t move in a straight line up,
but is just the beginning of the next Industrial Revolution as we move from  R&D/compute buildout into commercialization from Agents -> Physical AI -> discovery.

text:
$SIVE is my favorite CPO / photonics stock after AAOI.

Partly because it's Swedish and you have entertainment from comedians over there.

Today a new non-technical hedge fund called Protean Funds (likely shorting), went on air.

To said $SIVE CPO applications are imaginary.

Right after $GFS just made $SIVE their reference laser.

(Just for some context to newer readers: Lot of people in Sweden can only look at past 12 month revenue, and don't understand concepts of forward growth)

Also because they don't understand that no CPO application has scaled up yet at all.

So Swedish hedge funds keep going short (with many of their hedge funds like Colosseum / Origo heavily underwater).

But... for the technical readers... from H2 2026 to 2028, it goes from near $0 to $91B TAM in 1 1/2 years. (we're entering H2 now).

Overall TAM hits $141B (which is also 10x+ or so in 1 1/2 years)... and $SIVE has scaled into pluggable market with $JBL + other unnamed pluggable players with that too.

Probably not going to end well for the local Swedish firms, shorting right before the largest inflection points ever hits for $SIVE.

Just a matter of time before volume ramps.

text: While I agree that sector rotation is occurring in the market, I would have to disagree regarding the healthcare sector. The tech sector remains the hottest space and continues to offer excellent opportunities, with rotation happening internally (from semiconductors to SaaS, then IaaS, and now photonics). Just consider the catastrophic impact on global markets if the kind of rotation you’re describing were to actually occur, with trillions of dollars reallocating elsewhere… That would be nothing short of Armageddon.

text:
Took some painkillers and drew something on the Laser Photonics 1H chart with my magic crayons. 

Longer term calls for $LASE trade a much lower premium than short term calls, and I don't mean the usual shift near expiry. 

In my view this means the market does not "trust" the rally yet and considers it a pump. I'm reading the same across X as well. I can't help but wonder if all the volume we've seen over the past days has been short covers and retail traders. Especially since the short volume already dropped off relatively hard beforehand. 

Good. Let's see who's right. My hallucinatory mind or the market. 

That being said, please control your risk accordingly if you do choose to trade micro-cap companies.

text: Compute is going to keep improving. Today’s best matmul machines are nowhere near the physical limits of AI accelerators. There’s a lot of room to get better at digital silicon. There are also many candidates for new substrates, and the algorithmic debt they owe will be automated to its limits, but we don’t yet know what the optimal one is for AI in space/energy/time/manufacturability/cost. Photonics and stochastic silicon are both interesting candidates, but I also expect the singularity to be surprising.

text:
是否要增持AAOI,可以利用短期内，市场对 AAOI 的定价逻辑非常纯粹：你交得出多少货，我就给你多少估值。
1. 盯紧月度/季度产能释放节奏
 AAOI 在 2026 年底前要实现月产能从 10 万个到 65.5 万个的几何级跨越。
 短期内必须重点关注其休斯顿等厂区的设备到位率、良品率（Yield Rate）以及招工进度**。如果 800G 的出货量能在二季度和三季度环比连续翻倍，股价将在右侧获得最强的基本面支撑。
2. 验证扭亏为盈（Turnaround）的成色
 Q2 净利润是否顺利转正（EPS 跨过 0 美元生死线），以及毛利是否因为 800G 占比提升而显著拉高。
 只要 Q2 财报确认公司告别微亏，主流机构资金（Long-only 基金）就会开始将该股纳入常规配置池，带来一波估值中枢的系统性抬升。
3. 利用杠杆工具捕获短期动量
 * 鉴于当前处于 2026 Computex 催化剂的右侧爆发期，AAOG、AAOX、LNOK 这类 2 倍做多工具在短期内的动量（Momentum）极强，非常适合用来捕捉这波由短期产能预期引爆的暴力反弹。
🔭 远期前瞻布局：1.6T 切换与硅光子生态（2026 年底 - 2027 年）
当短期的业绩兑现后，股价要打开进一步的空间，就必须依赖远期叙事（Narrative）的接力。这时候，我们需要把目光从 AAOI 单个公司，放大到图中的 **EUV（半导体光子学 ETF）** 这一生态链上。
```
【远期技术演进路径】
 800G 可插拔光模块 (当前主力) 
       └───> 1.6T 高速率切换 (2026年底爆发)
                 └───> 硅光子 & CPO 架构 (未来的终极形态)

1. 1.6T 时代的先发卡位战
 * 随着下一代 AI 集群架构在 2026 年底逐步揭幕，**1.6T 光模块将正式进入样品测试和前期导入阶段**。
 * 远期我们需要验证 AAOI 的**高功率窄线宽泵浦激光器**能否顺利导入头部大客户。谁能率先在 1.6T 规模量产上卡位，谁就能拿走下一阶段最肥美的利润。
2. 硅光子与 CPO（共同封装光学）的临界点
 * 随着传输速率迈向 1.6T 甚至更高，传统的可插拔光模块将遭遇功耗和散热的物理瓶颈。**硅光子技术（Silicon Photonics）将从“备选项”变为“必选项”**。
 * 远期布局需要紧盯 **EUV ETF** 中那些掌握核心光芯片设计、激光器制造以及先进封装（如 TSMC 的 COWAS 衍生技术）的底层硬科技公司。这是行业规模从百亿级向千亿级跃迁的核心引擎。
策略执行落地建议
| 阶段 | 核心交易工具 | 核心盯盘信号 | 风险防控 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **短期（当前 - Q3）** | AAOI、AAOG、AAOX | 季报 EPS 转正、800G 月产能爬坡斜率 | 警惕大客户季度订单回调、杠杆损耗 |
| **远期（2026年底 - 2027）** | EUV ETF、AAOI（长线底仓） | 1.6T 样品通过率、硅光子产业链标准化进度 | 技术路径切换失败、行业竞争加剧 |
在这种“短看产能、远看技术”的框架下，短期冲锋用高弹性的个股或杠杆 ETF 咬住动量，远期用主题型 ETF（如 EUV）去包容技术路径的不确定性，是攻守兼备的最佳配置方式。

text: $SIVE $SIVEF insider selling is not a big deal. The ~$10M wireless MD sale + March cluster was mostly retiring board member Duffy (retirement) & non-CPO directors. CEO Vickram bought shares in MARCH. ZERO sales from CEO/CFO/COO or any CPO/photonics leadership (all locked up).  $LITE & $COHR have had  massive insider selling, almost zero net buys yet both ripped higher on AI/CPO momentum. Routine profit-taking in story stocks. Bull case intact.

text:
Hybrid optoelectronic AI computing architecture claims no active cooling needed. More here: https://t.co/qSWCD7KAEC

Artilux has introduced Inception, a hybrid optoelectronic architecture the company is targeting at next-generation AI computing, with the goal of improving power and area efficiency versus conventional digital electronics.

The company is aiming Inception at AI workloads where scaling pressure shows up as higher energy use, larger chips, heavier data movement, and harder thermal management. Inception combines photonics and electronics in a unified system, built around what Artilux describes as an optoelectronics-enabled hybrid systolic array architecture that is compatible with existing CPU, GPU, TPU, and LPU systems. Artilux says the architecture is intended to run large-scale general matrix–matrix multiplication (GEMM) with no data skew and negligible propagation delay.

More here: https://t.co/qSWCD7KAEC Artilux has introduced Inception, a hybrid optoelectronic architecture the company is targeting at next-generation AI computing, with the goal of improving power and area efficiency versus conventional digital electronics. The company is aiming Inception at AI workloads where scaling pressure shows up as higher energy use, larger chips, heavier data movement, and harder thermal management. Inception combines photonics and electronics in a unified system, built around what Artilux describes as an optoelectronics-enabled hybrid systolic array architecture that is compatible with existing CPU, GPU, TPU, and LPU systems. text:
The Conference Program for IEEE Summer Topicals 2026 is now available.

Join leading researchers, industry professionals, and innovators in Tulum, Mexico, for an engaging series of focused topical meetings covering emerging areas of photonics research and technology.

Featured plenary speakers:
• Natalie Wheeler, University of Southampton, UK
• Logan Wright, Yale University, USA

Register by 12 June to receive advanced rates, and reserve your hotel room by 10 June to qualify for discounted conference registration.

We look forward to seeing you in Mexico.

https://t.co/ULo6ZoORni
